edge_hypothesis: PF improves monotonically with R:R for MACD: 3.5:1 -> PF=1.347, 5:1 -> PF=1.353, 8:1 -> PF=1.442. The advisory explicitly recommends testing 12:1 R:R as the next logical boundary (directive #15). The mathematical reasoning: at 12:1 R:R with 1.0x ATR stop and 12.0x ATR TP, breakeven win rate is only 7.7%. The system achieves 18.59% win rate at 8:1 — even if win rate drops by half (to ~9%) at 12:1 R:R, it would still be profitable. The key question: does crypto produce 12x ATR moves on 4h? On ETH/4h, 1 ATR is roughly $50-80. A 12x ATR move would be $600-960 — major regime transitions (e.g., breakout from consolidation, capitulation events, FOMC-driven moves) can easily produce this. The MACD histogram zero-cross catches exactly these regime transitions because the histogram crossing zero means momentum has fully reversed direction. This is not just a pullback — it's a structural momentum shift. If the fat tail hypothesis holds at 12:1, it implies crypto returns have power-law characteristics where extreme moves are more common than normal distributions predict. This has profound implications for position sizing and risk management. Also testing on ETH/1h since the 8:1 ultra was validated on 1h (PF=1.442, 156 trades) — more signals on 1h means more opportunities to catch these extreme moves. Invalidation: (1) if 0 trades hit 12x ATR TP, crypto 4h trends are bounded below 12x ATR; (2) if PF < 1.0, the win rate degradation from 8:1 to 12:1 exceeds the R:R improvement — there's a cliff in the fat tail distribution.

variants.name: macd_12to1_ultimate_4h
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/4h with 12:1 R:R (1.0x stop / 12.0x TP). The ultimate tail harvester — tests whether the monotonic PF improvement continues beyond 8:1. Uses 1.0x ATR stop (proven viable at 8:1 with DD=7.1%) to keep losses minimal. 12.0x ATR TP targets major regime transitions. At breakeven win rate of 7.7%, even massive win rate degradation from 18.59% at 8:1 still leaves significant margin.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from <= 0.0 to > 0.0 (histogram zero-line cross bullish).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from >= 0.0 to < 0.0 (histogram zero-line cross bearish).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (12.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (1.0x ATR).
R:R = 12:1. Needs 7.7% win rate to break even after fees.
Hypothesis failure: if 0 trades hit TP, crypto 4h moves are bounded below 12x ATR
risk_rules: Stop loss at 1.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 12.0x ATR_14 from entry.

variants.name: macd_12to1_safe_stop_4h
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/4h with 8:1 R:R using wider stop (1.5x stop / 12.0x TP). Same 12x ATR TP target as the ultimate variant, but with the standard 1.5x safety-floor stop. This gives the trade more room to breathe and should produce a higher win rate. At 1.5x stop / 12.0x TP, R:R = 8:1 and breakeven win rate is 11.1%. This variant separates two variables: is the 12x ATR TP achievable (tested by both variants), and does stop width matter at extreme R:R (tested by comparing this variant vs the 1.0x stop variant).

variants.name: macd_12to1_1h_crossval
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross on ETH/1h with 12:1 R:R (1.0x stop / 12.0x TP). Cross-timeframe validation of the 12:1 R:R concept. The 8:1 ultra was validated on 1h (PF=1.442, 156 trades), so 1h is proven to work for MACD extreme R:R. On 1h, ATR is smaller so 12x ATR represents a proportionally smaller absolute move — more achievable than on 4h. If the fat tail hypothesis is timeframe-invariant, 12:1 should work on both 4h and 1h. 1h provides more signals (more MACD zero-crosses per month) so this is the higher-trade-count variant.
